content.text:
<user_query>
Audit frontend calibration flow files (`frontend/src/hooks/useCalibrationState.js`, `frontend/src/components/calibration/ChiefMarketingSummary.js`, related components) for expected payload keys. Report where strict JSON keys, sources mapping, unknown markers, ai_errors, and gap visibility must be added or adjusted to avoid placeholder rendering and preserve evidence.
</user_query>
Auditing the calibration flow: reading the hook, ChiefMarketingSummary, and related calibration components for payload keys, sources, unknown markers, ai_errors, and gap visibility.
Searching for payload keys, `ai_errors`, unknown markers, and sources usage across calibration files.

Here is an audit focused on **payload keys**, **sources**, **unknown/sentinel handling**, **`ai_errors`**, and **gap visibility**, and how they relate to **placeholder-looking UI** and **evidence**. --- ## 1. `useCalibrationState.js` — payload assembly and contracts ### Primary scan path (`handleAuditSubmit`) **What is merged well** - Builds `ex` from `auditData.extracted_data` plus optional `deepEnrichment`, social, deep recon, competitor monitor. - Persists traceability on `_full`: `_sources` (DNA `data_sources` string list), `_deep_sources`, `_social_sources`, `_deep_recon_sources`, `_trace`, `_field_provenance`, `analysis_gaps`, `market_intelligence_score`, `market_trajectory`, `market_evidence`, etc. **Gaps / adjustments** | Concern | Issue | |--------|--------| | **Strict / consistent keys** | Edge DNA’s prompt schema includes `services_offered` (array) alongside `main_products_services`. The hook only maps WOW/`what_you_do` from `main_products_services` (and alts like `description`, `about`). If the model fills **`services_offered` only**, the UI can look empty unless you **normalize** on the client (or fix the edge to always map into `main_products_services`). | | **Per-field `sources` map** | DNA asks the model for `sources: { field: [urls] }` inside `extracted_data`. The hook does not copy that into a dedicated `_field_sources` (or similar); it may remain on `ex.sources` but **ChiefMarketingSummary never shows it**. Evidence URLs are easy to lose visually. | | **`ai_errors` / parse / AI fallback** | Backend enrichment can log AI/parse issues (`calibration.py` around synthesis/parse). The hook **does not read or store** any structured `ai_errors` or “partial synthesis” flag on `_full`, so the CMO screen cannot distinguish **real scan signal** from **deterministic fallback text** from the API. | | **`market_evidence`** | It is merged onto `ex` (and provenance list) but **no downstream component in this flow renders it** — evidence is dropped for the user. | | **Regenerate path (`handleRegenerateIdentity`)** | Rebuilds `wow` / `_full` from DNA only: `_sources` + `_website` + `_generated_at`. It **does not** re-run deep enrichment, so **`analysis_gaps`, `market_intelligence_score`, `market_evidence`, `_deep_sources`, channel diagnostics, etc. disappear** compared to the first scan. That often produces **generic “unknown / n/a” blocks** on the CMO page even though the user still has a valid DNA extract. | | **Manual summary (`handleManualSummary`)** | Sets a minimal `wowSummary` with **no `_full`**, no sources, no gaps. Chief path then has almost no structured evidence (expected to some degree, but worth surfacing explicitly in UI as “manual entry — no scan evidence”). | | **Save payload (`handleConfirmWow`)** | `cmoBundle` includes `deep_scan_sources` from `full.deep_scan_sources || full._deep_sources` but **not** `_sources` / `_social_sources` / `_deep_recon_sources` / per-field `sources`. Persisted `competitor_scan_result` JSON is **incomplete as a citation bundle**. | --- ## 2. `ChiefMarketingSummary.js` — rendering vs payload **Uses `full` from `wowSummary._full` (or flat fallback)** for: business copy, layer scores, executive brief, website/SEO/paid/social, SWOT, competitor SWOT, provenance, gaps, trace. **Where placeholders or weak evidence appear** | Area | Problem | |------|--------| | **Channel diagnostics** | `websiteHealth.summary` absent → **`Status: unknown · Score: n/a`**; SEO **`unknown` / `n/a`**. That reads as placeholder even when the real issue is “enrichment object empty” or “regenerate path skipped enrichment”. | | **Market presence `LAYERS`** | Several layers key off **`competitive_moat`**, **`mission_statement`**, etc., while the scan pipeline often emphasizes **`competitive_advantages`**, **`main_products_services`**, **`unique_value_proposition`**. Layers can show **Partial/Unavailable** while other sections show real text — looks inconsistent, not strictly “wrong”. | | **`buildCompetitorInsights`** | Uses `full.competitor_analysis` (string). DNA supplies that; deep path also fills narrative via other keys. If only **`competitor_scan_result`**-shaped data exists without `competitor_analysis`, the **“Competitive Pressure Summary”** block can be empty while competitor names exist. | | **SWOT block** | Renders when `swot` is non-empty; each quadrant shows **only the first array item**, else **“No verified data yet.”** — strong placeholder tone if the API sends empty arrays or single generic strings. | | **`analysis_gaps` / intelligence quality** | Section only renders if **`marketIntelScore !== null || analysisGaps.length > 0`**. If enrichment did not run or returned no score and empty gaps, **users see no “gaps” panel at all** — opposite of “preserve evidence” when the scan was shallow. A neutral **“public scan only; no scored gaps”** state would be clearer. | | **`market_evidence`** | **Never read** — should be wired into “Intelligence Quality” or its own evidence list. | | **Sources** | **`ExecutiveCMOSnapshot`** (`IntelligencePhases.js`) shows `intelligenceData.data_sources`; **ChiefMarketingSummary does not show** `_sources`, `_deep_sources`, `_social_sources`, `_deep_recon_sources`, or **`extracted_data.sources`**. Users cannot see **which URLs supported each claim** on the footprint report. | | **Unknown markers** | Only a few places treat **`Not available from current data`** (layer scoring, pricing audit). Other sentinels or empty objects still flow into narrative strings from backend fallbacks (`calibration.py` builds default `executive_summary`, `market_position`, etc.) and can read as confident copy unless paired with **gap / provenance** cues. | | **`ai_errors`** | **Not referenced** — no banner or footnote when synthesis failed server-side. | --- ## 3. Related components (short) - **`WowSummary.js`** — Only lists **string** fields on `wowSummary`, so `_full` is not dumped into the card list. Good. It does **not** show sources or gaps (by design); the risk is mostly **regenerate/manual** shrinking content with no explanation. - **`IntelligencePhases.js` (`ExecutiveCMOSnapshot`)** — Correctly uses top-level **`data_sources`** from `biqc-insights-cognitive` and **`cognitive.data_gaps`** (different shape from calibration **`analysis_gaps`**). Still **no `ai_errors`**; parse failure is folded into a generic cognitive object on the edge. - **`ForensicIdentityCard.js` / `parseIdentitySignals`** — Identity-only; no source URLs or AI error state. **`useCalibrationState`** already avoids promoting **`Not available from current data`** into ABN/address for signals; align any new sentinels the same way. - **`CalibrationAdvisor.js`** — If `wowSummary` is missing, it falls back to **`ManualSummaryFallback`** — again **no evidence trail**. --- ## 4. Recommended direction (by theme) 1. **Strict / predictable keys (client normalization)** After merge, map **`services_offered` → `main_products_services`** (join/summary), and align **`competitive_moat` ↔ competitive narrative** if the API only sends `competitive_advantages`. 2. **Sources mapping (evidence)** Surface on CMO (and optionally in `cmoBundle`): **`_sources`**, **`_deep_sources`**, **`_social_sources`**, **`_deep_recon_sources`**, and **`extracted_data.sources`** (field → URLs), even as a collapsible “Citations” section. 3. **Unknown / partial markers** Treat **empty `{}`**, **`unknown`/`n/a` diagnostics**, and **backend template sentences** as **low-confidence**: short label + link to gaps, not full paragraph tone. 4. **`ai_errors` / synthesis status** Have the API return a small **`enrichment_meta: { ai_errors[], partial: boolean }`** (or reuse an existing field) and have the hook stash it on `_full`; CMO shows a **non-blocking** line when present. 5. **Gap visibility** Always show an **intelligence quality** strip: either **`analysis_gaps`**, or explicit **“No gap list — scan-only / enrichment skipped”** (e.g. after regenerate without re-enrichment). 6. **`market_evidence`** Render next to **`market_intelligence_score` / `market_trajectory`** so scores are anchored to **observable evidence**. 7. **Regenerate parity** Either **re-invoke** `/calibration/enrichment/website` (or equivalent) on regenerate, or **preserve** the previous `_full` enrichment fields when regenerating identity-only so the CMO does not **collapse** to placeholder diagnostics. --- ## 5. Code anchors (for navigation) Hook: merge and `_full` assembly (sources, gaps, `market_evidence`): ```536:546:C:\Users\andre\.cursor\worktrees\tpn\ukf\frontend\src\hooks\useCalibrationState.js const fullExtraction = { ...ex, _sources: auditData.data_sources || [], _deep_sources: deepEnrichment?.sources || null, _social_sources: socialEnrichment?.sources || null, _deep_recon_sources: deepReconData?.sources || null, _website: url, _generated_at: auditData.generated_at || new Date().toISOString(), _trace: calibrationTraceRef.current, _field_provenance: fieldProvenanceRef.current, }; ``` Regenerate path — thin `_full`: ```712:714:C:\Users\andre\.cursor\worktrees\tpn\ukf\frontend\src\hooks\useCalibrationState.js const fullExtraction = { ...ex, _sources: auditData.data_sources || [], _website: url, _generated_at: new Date().toISOString() }; ``` CMO: diagnostics fallbacks and gap section gate: ```431:439:C:\Users\andre\.cursor\worktrees\tpn\ukf\frontend\src\components\calibration\ChiefMarketingSummary.js

{(websiteHealth.summary || `Status: ${websiteHealth.status || 'unknown'} · Score: ${websiteHealth.score ?? 'n/a'}`)}

... Score {seoAnalysis.score ?? 'n/a'} · Status {seoAnalysis.status || 'unknown'} ``` ```754:777:C:\Users\andre\.cursor\worktrees\tpn\ukf\frontend\src\components\calibration\ChiefMarketingSummary.js {(marketIntelScore !== null || analysisGaps.length > 0) && ( ... {analysisGaps.length > 0 ? ( ... ) : (

No critical public-data gaps detected in this scan cycle.

)} ``` Snapshot sources (contrast: CMO has no equivalent): ```345:352:C:\Users\andre\.cursor\worktrees\tpn\ukf\frontend\src\components\calibration\IntelligencePhases.js {sources.length > 0 && (
Sources: {sources.map((s, i) => ( ``` --- I’m in **Ask mode**, so this is an audit only; if you want these wired in the repo, switch to **Agent mode** and we can implement in a tight, file-scoped way.
